entities.name: CHICKPEA
entities.description: Cultivated Cicer arietinum L. used in improvement programs

entities.name: WILD CICER SPECIES
entities.description: Annual wild species of the Cicer genus used for chickpea improvement and conservation

entities.name: PHOTOPERIOD
entities.description: The duration of light exposure which affects plant phenology, growth, and yield

entities.name: VERNALIZATION
entities.description: Temperature sensitivity requiring cold treatment for flowering

entities.name: PHENOLOGY
entities.description: The timing of biological events in the plant life cycle

entities.name: GROWTH
entities.description: The physical development and increase in size of the plant

entities.name: DAYS TO FIRST FLOWERING
entities.description: The number of days from planting until the first flower appears

entities.name: YIELD-RELATED TRAITS
entities.description: Agronomic characteristics that determine the final crop yield

entities.name: POD NUMBER
entities.description: The total number of pods produced per plant

entities.name: SEED YIELD PER PLANT
entities.description: The total amount of seed produced by an individual plant

entities.name: VEGETATIVE PHASE
entities.description: The initial growth stage of the plant focused on leaf and stem development

entities.name: REPRODUCTIVE PHASE
entities.description: The stage of plant growth focused on flowering and seed production

entities.name: REGENERATION
entities.description: The process of renewing plant materials for conservation in genebanks

entities.name: HYBRIDIZATION
entities.description: The process of crossing different genetic lines to improve crop traits

entities.name: GENEBANKS
entities.description: Facilities used for the conservation of plant genetic resources

entities.name: C. RETICULATUM
entities.description: A wild Cicer species that responds best to an 18h photoperiod for regeneration and reducing the vegetative phase

entities.name: C. JUDAICUM
entities.description: A wild Cicer species that responds best to a 15h photoperiod

entities.name: C. YAMASHITAE
entities.description: A wild Cicer species that responds best to a 15h photoperiod

entities.name: C. BIJUGUM
entities.description: A wild Cicer species that responds to 15h for regeneration and 18h for reducing the vegetative phase

entities.name: C. PINNATIFIDUM
entities.description: A wild Cicer species that responds to 15h for regeneration and 18h for reducing the vegetative phase

entities.name: C. CHORASSANICUM
entities.description: A wild Cicer species showing weak response to extended photoperiod treatments

entities.name: C. CUNEATUM
entities.description: A wild Cicer species showing weak response to extended photoperiod treatments
